wiki_catalogs.name: Project Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the Sky News Interactive Display project overview. Explain the project's purpose as a full-stack multimedia application for broadcast environments, its core functionality including voice-activated news queries, AI-powered content generation, and real-time video presentation. Document the target audience (broadcast newsrooms, interactive displays), key features (voice interaction, AI news generation, multi-modal presentation), and technology stack (FastAPI, Next.js, WebSocket). Include architectural overview showing the relationship between frontend display interface, tablet control interface, backend services, and external APIs. Provide practical examples of user workflows and system capabilities.

wiki_catalogs.name: REST API Endpoints
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive REST API documentation for Sky News Interactive Display. Document all HTTP endpoints including /api/health (GET), /api/news/search (POST), and /api/video/search (POST). For each endpoint, specify HTTP methods, URL patterns, request/response schemas, authentication requirements, and status codes. Include detailed parameter descriptions, example requests/responses, error handling patterns, and rate limiting information. Document the news generation API for structured content creation and video search API for YouTube integration. Provide practical examples demonstrating common use cases and integration patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Display Interface
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Sky News Display Interface component. Document the main display page architecture, state management for avatar states, news presentation, and video integration. Explain the responsive layout system with left/right panel design, avatar animation states (idle, listen, think, present), and content transition effects. Cover the NewsCard component for displaying structured news data, VideoPlayer integration for YouTube content, and StatusBar for connection status. Include detailed coverage of React state hooks, WebSocket message handling, conditional rendering logic, and responsive design patterns. Provide examples of component props, state transitions, and user interaction flows.

wiki_catalogs.name: Application Entry Point
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the FastAPI application entry point. Document the application initialization, CORS middleware configuration, router registration, and development server setup. Explain the modular routing architecture with WebSocket and news routers. Cover the application structure, middleware setup, and production deployment considerations. Include examples of how to run the application locally and configure it for different environments.

wiki_catalogs.name: Getting Started
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive getting started documentation for Sky News Interactive Display. Include prerequisites (Node.js, Python, environment setup), step-by-step installation instructions for both frontend and backend, environment variable configuration, and initial setup procedures. Document system requirements, dependency installation, API key configuration for DashScope and YouTube, and first-run verification steps. Provide clear instructions for running development servers, accessing the display and tablet interfaces, and basic troubleshooting for common setup issues. Include practical examples of successful installation verification.

wiki_catalogs.name: WebSocket API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed WebSocket API documentation for Sky News Interactive Display. Document connection protocols, message formats, event types, and real-time interaction patterns. Explain session management, connection handling, and message broadcasting mechanisms. Detail the standardized message schema, state transition events, and avatar communication protocols. Include connection lifecycle management, error handling strategies, reconnection logic, and debugging approaches. Provide client implementation examples showing how to establish connections, handle messages, manage sessions, and synchronize state across multiple clients.

wiki_catalogs.name: Tablet Interface
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the Sky News Tablet Interface component. Document the tablet control interface architecture, speech recognition implementation, and microphone button functionality. Explain the MicButton component with speech recognition APIs, StatusIndicator for connection and processing states, and TranscriptBox for displaying recognized speech. Cover the integration with browser SpeechRecognition API, real-time speech processing, error handling for audio input, and user feedback mechanisms. Include examples of speech recognition configuration, event handling, state management for recording states, and accessibility considerations for voice-enabled interfaces.

wiki_catalogs.name: WebSocket Router
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the WebSocket router implementation. Document the WebSocket endpoint handling, session management, message processing pipeline, and state machine orchestration. Explain the connection lifecycle, message broadcasting mechanisms, and real-time communication patterns. Cover error handling, connection cleanup, and performance optimization. Include examples of WebSocket message formats, state transitions, and integration with the avatar animation system.

wiki_catalogs.name: Architecture Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create architectural documentation for Sky News Interactive Display system architecture. Describe the high-level design showing the relationship between frontend React application, backend FastAPI server, WebSocket communication layer, and external AI/media services. Document component interactions, data flow patterns, and integration architecture. Explain the dual-interface design (display and tablet), real-time communication patterns, and state management across components. Include system boundaries, technology stack decisions, and architectural patterns used (Observer, Command, Factory). Address scalability considerations, deployment topology, and cross-cutting concerns like security and monitoring.

wiki_catalogs.name: Shared Utilities and Constants
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the shared frontend utilities and constants used across Sky News components. Document the constants.ts file containing TypeScript interfaces, message types, avatar states, and configuration values. Explain the useSession custom hook for WebSocket connection management, session handling, and real-time state synchronization. Cover the websocket utility functions for connection establishment, message formatting, and error handling. Include detailed coverage of the WSMessage interface, AvatarState enum, MESSAGE_TYPES constants, and NewsData/VideoData interfaces. Provide examples of how these utilities integrate with React components, handle connection lifecycle, and manage application state across different views.

wiki_catalogs.name: News API Endpoints
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for the news-related endpoints. Document the health check endpoint, news search API, and video search API with their respective HTTP methods, request/response schemas, and error handling. Explain the RESTful design patterns, parameter validation, and response formatting. Include practical examples of API usage, authentication requirements, and integration patterns with the frontend components.

wiki_catalogs.name: Backend System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive backend system documentation for Sky News Interactive Display. Document the FastAPI application architecture, WebSocket communication handling, REST API endpoints, and service layer implementation. Explain the modular design with separate services for news generation, video fetching, and WebSocket management. Cover configuration management, environment-based settings, and integration with external APIs (DashScope, YouTube). Include detailed coverage of the WebSocket state machine, message processing, session management, and real-time communication patterns. Document error handling, logging, and performance considerations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Service Layer
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the backend service layer architecture. Document the news generation service with DashScope API integration, structured content generation, and confidence scoring. Explain the video fetching service with YouTube search algorithms, embed URL generation, and metadata extraction. Cover the WebSocket manager for connection pooling, session coordination, and message broadcasting. Include service initialization, dependency injection, error handling, and performance considerations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Frontend System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive frontend system documentation for Sky News Interactive Display. Document the Next.js application structure, dual-interface design (display and tablet), component architecture, and real-time state management. Explain the React component hierarchy, TypeScript interfaces, and WebSocket integration patterns. Cover the avatar animation system, news card presentation, video player integration, and tablet speech recognition interface. Include detailed coverage of component state management, user interaction patterns, responsive design considerations, and performance optimization techniques.

wiki_catalogs.name: Data Models and Schemas
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for the backend data models and schemas. Document the Pydantic models for news responses, video results, and WebSocket messages. Explain the data validation rules, field definitions, and serialization patterns. Cover the relationship between models and their usage in API endpoints and services. Include examples of data transformation, validation errors, and schema evolution strategies.

wiki_catalogs.name: API Reference
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for Sky News Interactive Display. Document all REST API endpoints including health check, news search, and video search APIs with HTTP methods, URL patterns, request/response schemas, and authentication requirements. Provide detailed WebSocket API documentation covering connection protocols, message formats, event types, state transitions, and real-time interaction patterns. Include protocol-specific examples, error handling strategies, rate limiting considerations, and client implementation guidelines. Document common use cases, debugging approaches, and integration patterns for both REST and WebSocket communications.

wiki_catalogs.name: Configuration Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the backend configuration management system. Document environment-based settings, API key management, and runtime configuration options. Explain the configuration loading mechanism, default values, and environment variable handling. Cover security considerations for API keys, development vs production settings, and configuration validation. Include examples of setting up different environments and managing sensitive configuration data.

wiki_catalogs.name: Data Models and Schemas
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive data model documentation for Sky News Interactive Display. Detail the backend data schemas including NewsData, VideoData, and WebSocket message structures with field definitions, data types, and validation rules. Document the frontend TypeScript interfaces for NewsData, VideoData, and WSMessage with their properties and usage patterns. Explain entity relationships, data transformation between layers, and consistency requirements. Include schema evolution considerations, backward compatibility, and data validation strategies. Provide examples of data flow between components and integration patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Deployment and Operations
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive deployment and operations documentation for Sky News Interactive Display. Document development environment setup, local development workflow, and production deployment strategies. Cover containerization options, reverse proxy configuration, load balancing considerations, and scaling approaches. Include monitoring and logging setup, health check implementation, and operational metrics collection. Document CI/CD pipeline integration, automated deployment processes, and rollback procedures. Address security considerations, backup strategies, and disaster recovery planning for broadcast environments.

wiki_catalogs.name: Troubleshooting and FAQ
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive troubleshooting and FAQ documentation for Sky News Interactive Display. Document common issues including WebSocket connection problems, AI API integration failures, video loading errors, and frontend component issues. Provide systematic debugging approaches, diagnostic commands, and log analysis techniques. Include performance optimization guidance for memory management, connection pooling, and caching strategies. Address network connectivity issues, API quota limitations, and external service dependencies. Document error codes, resolution steps, and preventive measures for recurring problems.

wiki_overview.content:
<blog>

# Sky News Interactive Display - Comprehensive Project Analysis

## 1. Project Introduction

### Purpose Statement
Sky News Interactive Display is a sophisticated multimedia news presentation system that combines voice interaction, AI-powered news generation, and real-time video streaming to create an immersive news experience for display screens and tablet interfaces.

### Core Goals and Objectives
- **Real-time Voice Interaction**: Enable users to ask questions about current events through voice commands
- **AI-Powered News Generation**: Leverage advanced language models to generate structured news content
- **Multi-modal Presentation**: Combine visual avatars, news cards, and embedded videos for engaging presentations
- **Interactive Display System**: Support both standalone display screens and tablet-based control interfaces

### Target Audience
- **Primary**: Newsrooms and broadcast centers requiring interactive news displays
- **Secondary**: Educational institutions and corporate environments seeking modern news presentation solutions
- **Technical Users**: Developers and IT professionals managing interactive media systems

## 2. Technical Architecture

The project follows a modern microservices architecture with clear separation between frontend and backend components:

```mermaid
graph TD
    A[Tablet Interface] --> B[WebSocket Server]
    C[Display Interface] --> B
    B --> D[FastAPI Backend]
    D --> E[News Engine]
    D --> F[Video Fetcher]
    E --> G[DashScope API]
    F --> H[YouTube API]
    B --> I[Connection Manager]
    I --> J[Session Management]
```

### Component Breakdown

**Frontend Layer**:
- **Next.js Application**: React-based frontend with TypeScript support
- **Dual Interface Design**: Separate interfaces for tablet control and display monitoring
- **Real-time State Management**: WebSocket-driven communication for live updates

**Backend Layer**:
- **FastAPI Server**: High-performance Python web framework
- **WebSocket Communication**: Real-time bidirectional messaging
- **Service-Oriented Architecture**: Modular services for different functionalities

**Integration Layer**:
- **AI Model Integration**: DashScope API for news generation
- **Media Services**: YouTube integration for video content
- **Configuration Management**: Environment-based settings

### Design Patterns

The system implements several key design patterns:

- **Observer Pattern**: WebSocket connections observe state changes
- **Command Pattern**: Message-based communication between components
- **Factory Pattern**: Dynamic message creation and processing
- **State Machine**: Avatar states and session lifecycle management

## 3. Key Implementation

### Main Entry Points

**Backend Entry Point** (`backend/main.py`):
- Initializes FastAPI application with CORS middleware
- Configures routing for WebSocket and REST endpoints
- Sets up development server with hot reloading

**Frontend Entry Point** (`frontend/src/app/display/page.tsx`):
- Primary display interface with responsive layout
- Real-time state synchronization with backend
- Component-based architecture for modular UI

### Core Modules

**WebSocket Communication** (`backend/routers/ws.py`):
- Session-based connection management
- Multi-client broadcasting capabilities
- Real-time state synchronization

**News Generation Engine** (`backend/services/news_engine.py`):
- AI-powered news content generation using DashScope
- Structured JSON response validation
- Fallback mechanisms for error handling

**Video Integration** (`backend/services/video_fetcher.py`):
- YouTube search and embed URL generation
- Thumbnail extraction and metadata processing
- Robust error handling and fallback strategies

### Configuration Approach

**Environment Configuration** (`backend/config.py`):
- Pydantic-based settings management
- Environment variable loading
- Type-safe configuration access

**Frontend Constants** (`frontend/src/lib/constants.ts`):
- Centralized message types and state definitions
- Timeout configurations for interactive behavior
- TypeScript interfaces for type safety

### External Dependencies

**Backend Dependencies** (`backend/requirements.txt`):
- FastAPI 0.115.0: High-performance web framework
- OpenAI 1.82.0: AI model integration
- Websockets 12.0: Real-time communication
- HTTPX 0.27.0: Asynchronous HTTP client

**Frontend Dependencies** (`frontend/package.json`):
- Next.js 16.2.9: React framework with TypeScript support
- Tailwind CSS 4: Utility-first CSS framework
- React 19.2.4: Component-based UI library

### Integration Points

**WebSocket Protocol** (`backend/routers/ws.py`):
- Standardized message format with type, session_id, and payload
- Session-based broadcasting for multi-client coordination
- Real-time state synchronization across connected devices

**API Endpoints** (`backend/routers/news.py`):
- RESTful endpoints for health checks and data retrieval
- Structured response models for consistent data exchange
- Error handling with descriptive HTTP status codes

### Component Relationships

```mermaid
graph LR
    A[Tablet Interface] --> B[WebSocket Client]
    B --> C[WebSocket Router]
    C --> D[Connection Manager]
    D --> E[News Pipeline]
    E --> F[News Engine]
    E --> G[Video Fetcher]
    F --> H[DashScope API]
    G --> I[YouTube API]
    C --> J[Display Interface]
    J --> K[Avatar Component]
    J --> L[News Card Component]
    J --> M[Video Player Component]
```

## 4. Key Features

### Functionality Overview

**Voice-Activated News System**:
- Real-time speech-to-text conversion using browser APIs
- Natural language processing for news queries
- Multi-stage avatar animation reflecting system states

**AI-Powered Content Generation**:
- Structured news content with headlines, summaries, and key facts
- Automated video search term generation
- Confidence scoring for content reliability

**Multi-Modal Presentation**:
- Animated avatar with four distinct states (idle, listen, think, present)
- Responsive news card displaying structured information
- Embedded YouTube player with automatic video selection

### Implementation Highlights

**State Management** (`frontend/src/app/display/page.tsx`):
- Context-aware state transitions based on avatar states
- Real-time transcript display during listening phases
- Smooth animations and transitions between states

**WebSocket Communication** (`backend/services/websocket_manager.py`):
- Session-based connection pooling
- Automatic cleanup of disconnected clients
- Reliable message delivery with error handling

**Error Handling and Resilience**:
- Graceful degradation when AI services are unavailable
- Fallback content generation with reduced functionality
- Automatic recovery from network interruptions

### Feature Architecture

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> Idle: Initial State
    Idle --> Listen: Voice Input Detected
    Listen --> Think: Speech Processed
    Think --> Present: Content Generated
    Present --> Idle: Timeout (30s)
    
    state Present {
        [*] --> NewsDisplay
        NewsDisplay --> VideoPlayback
        VideoPlayback --> [*]
    }
```

The system demonstrates sophisticated real-time communication, AI integration, and multi-modal presentation capabilities that create an engaging and informative news experience for modern digital environments.

---

**Sources:**
- [main.py](backend/main.py)
- [config.py](backend/config.py)
- [schemas.py](backend/models/schemas.py)
- [news.py](backend/routers/news.py)
- [news_engine.py](backend/services/news_engine.py)
- [video_fetcher.py](backend/services/video_fetcher.py)
- [page.tsx](frontend/src/app/display/page.tsx)
- [constants.ts](frontend/src/lib/constants.ts)
- [package.json](frontend/package.json)
- [requirements.txt](backend/requirements.txt)
- [Avatar.tsx](frontend/src/components/display/Avatar.tsx)
- [MicButton.tsx](frontend/src/components/tablet/MicButton.tsx)
- [ws.py](backend/routers/ws.py)
- [websocket_manager.py](backend/services/websocket_manager.py)

</blog>
